inline_comments.message:
Nb. This will likely give worse performance when using alternate Id now. Assuming we have an alternateId:
- first we try get YangModelCmHandle by id on line 285, which fails
- then we resolve the reference using getResolvedCmHandleId() which
  - tries to look up Cm handle by id, which fails again
  - tries to look up Cm handle by alternate-id, which succeeds and returns id
- get the YangModelCmHandle by resolved id

So this patch optimizes for Cm Handle ID - doing it in a single DB lookup, while it takes 4 DB lookups to resolve alternate ID!

I am investigating alternatives as part of CPS-2511

we agreed to Optimize for CM handle ID and NOT alternate ID...
that is the purpose of this patch as ESH still uses alternate IDs for data access

NB: this is not a good way of obtaining a bean from the context. Instead the class can be a service that autowire(injects) AlternateIdMatcher at field level. 

The consuming class is actively pulling its dependencies (getCpsBean) instead of having them pushed via dependency injection.
This breaks the IoC principle, making the architecture less aligned with Spring's design philosophy.

Since the urgency of the bug, that is ok.
